subject: [PATCH v2] nfsd: don't free session slots that are still in use

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Identified the issue and suggested a fix. The patch addresses both problems: checking slotid against se_target_maxslots, and scanning slots in the to-be-freed range for NFSD4_SLOT_INUSE.
reviews.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Tue, 26 May 2026 12:24:48 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:
> nfsd4_sequence() can free the very slot it is currently processing.
> When the session shrinker has reduced se_target_maxslots below
> se_fchannel.maxreqs, the shrink path checks three conditions before
> calling free_session_slots():
> 
>   1. se_target_maxslots < maxreqs  (shrink was advertised)
>   2. slot->sl_generation == se_slot_gen  (slot is up-to-date)
>   3. seq->maxslots <= se_target_maxslots  (client acknowledges)
> 
> [...]

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/1] nfsd: don't free session slots that are still in use
      commit: 6085718398d83e16ccdc864c209493671f3c5998

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch addresses a use-after-free vulnerability in the NFSv4 server by adding two checks to prevent freeing session slots that are still in use. The first check ensures that the current request's slotid is below the shrink boundary, and the second scan checks for active slots in the range to be freed.
